releases.summary: Harden the Builder rollout with canonical bootstrap presets, channel-heavy mobile review flows, local-daemon runtime registration, and richer operator status surfaces.

audiences.summary: Builder bootstrap presets, local-daemon registration, and mobile/operator status views now move together as one governed rollout surface.
Integrators can adopt canonical bootstrap presets, register trusted local daemons, and consume richer snapshot-backed mobile review and operator posture data.
Builder operators get clearer local runtime trust, lease, and channel-action status when reviewing rollouts away from the desktop workbench.

upgradeSteps.title: Verify hardened Builder bootstrap and local-daemon flows
upgradeSteps.summary: Confirm the new preset, mobile-review, and local runtime registration surfaces are wired through the workbench snapshot.
instructions: Update any bootstrap orchestration to use the managed, local-daemon, or hybrid preset values exposed by the Builder contracts.
Register a local daemon through the runtime integration and confirm the resulting trust and lease details appear in the Builder snapshot surfaces.
Review the mobile-review and operator status cards to verify channel-native actions and comparison posture are rendered from the shared snapshot model.

commands: cd packages/libs/builder-spec && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/libs/builder-runtime && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/modules/builder-workbench && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/modules/mobile-review && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/apps/cli-contractspec && bun test && bun run typecheck
evidence: Builder bootstrap contracts now expose canonical managed, local-daemon, and hybrid presets instead of leaving rollout posture to ad hoc caller logic.
Runtime-local registration and mobile review card resolution both feed the shared Builder snapshot so desktop and mobile/operator surfaces describe the same rollout state.
CLI and library-facing operator guidance now surface the richer local trust, lease, channel action, and comparison posture details introduced by the hardened rollout.

releases.summary: Introduce the Builder v3 control plane as a governed authoring layer over external execution providers.

audiences.summary: Builder v3 now has a governed contract, runtime, and provider surface that keeps authoring, readiness, and export orchestration aligned across the package stack.
Integrators can compose managed, local, and hybrid runtime modes with Builder workbench/mobile-review modules and provider adapters for Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Copilot, STT, and local models.
Builder operators now get a unified workbench and mobile-review experience across provider routing, readiness, export approval, and omnichannel control flows.

upgradeSteps.title: Adopt the Builder v3 control-plane packages
upgradeSteps.summary: Wire provider/runtime integrations through the governed Builder v3 workbench and mobile-review surfaces.
instructions: Install the Builder v3 contracts and runtime packages alongside the provider integrations you intend to expose in the authoring surface.
Use the Builder workbench and mobile-review modules as the host UI surfaces instead of building separate readiness or export orchestration shells.
Validate managed, local, and hybrid runtime mode selection plus provider routing before promoting the control-plane workflow to operators.

commands: cd packages/libs/builder-spec && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/libs/builder-runtime && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/modules/builder-workbench && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/modules/mobile-review && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/apps/web-landing && bun run build:types && bun test
evidence: Builder v3 contracts, runtime mode wrappers, and provider compatibility surfaces are now published together as one governed authoring layer.
The reusable workbench and mobile-review modules consume the same readiness, export, and provider-routing state that the runtime integrations expose across managed, local, and hybrid modes.
Omnichannel control integrations for Telegram, voice, and WhatsApp are aligned with the same control-plane package set instead of shipping as disconnected operator workflows.

releases.summary: Implement ContractSpec Connect as a first-class spec, runtime, and CLI workflow.

audiences.summary: Connect is now a governed repo surface with CLI commands, workspace services, and versioned docs that keep risky edits, review packets, and replay artifacts aligned.
Integrators can enable `.contractsrc.json > connect`, emit local context/plan/verdict artifacts, and route adapter-facing review or replay flows through the shared workspace services.

Coding-agent surfaces now have a first-class Connect workflow for context packs, plan packets, mutation verdicts, review packets, and replay/eval evidence instead of relying on ad hoc governance prose.

migrationInstructions.title: Enable ContractSpec Connect in the workspace config
migrationInstructions.summary: Turn on the Connect adapter flow before relying on task-scoped context, review, replay, or evaluation artifacts.
migrationInstructions.when: When a workspace wants coding-agent gating, local review packets, or replay/eval evidence tied to file and command mutations.
steps: Add or merge a `connect` section in `.contractsrc.json` with `enabled: true` and the storage paths that should hold Connect artifacts.
Route risky edits or shell execution through `contractspec connect plan` and `contractspec connect verify` so decisions, review packets, and replay bundles are captured.
Use the generated docs and exported agentpacks metadata so downstream agent tooling sees the same Connect contract surface as the CLI.

upgradeSteps.title: Adopt the Connect CLI workflow
upgradeSteps.summary: Use the built-in Connect commands instead of custom local wrappers for risky file or command mutations.
instructions: Initialize the workspace with `contractspec connect init --scope workspace`.
Use `contractspec connect context`, `plan`, and `verify` to capture task context and mutation verdicts before edits or shell execution.
Keep replay and evaluation artifacts under `.contractspec/connect/` so review and audit flows can consume the same evidence bundle.

commands: cd packages/libs/contracts-spec && bun test src/workspace-config/contractsrc-schema.test.ts src/versioning/schema.test.ts && bun run typecheck
cd packages/bundles/workspace && bun test src/services/connect/plan-policy.test.ts src/services/upgrade/guided-upgrade.test.ts src/services/versioning/release-service.test.ts && bun run typecheck
cd packages/apps/cli-contractspec && bun test src/commands/connect/blackbox.test.ts src/commands/connect/index.test.ts && bun run typecheck
cd packages/tools/agentpacks && bun test && bun run typecheck
evidence: ContractSpec Connect now ships as a first-class spec, workspace service, and CLI workflow instead of being split across unversioned helper paths.
Local context, plan, verdict, review, replay, and evaluation artifacts all resolve through the same `.contractsrc.json > connect` configuration and workspace storage layout.
Agent-facing docs and exported metadata now describe the same Connect workflow that the CLI and workspace services enforce.

releases.summary: Expand the spec-pack docs into a fuller learning path across the public docs site.

audiences.summary: The docs bundle and web app now expose Connect, module-bundle, and Builder workbench guides as one linked learning path instead of isolated pages.
Public docs readers can follow a clearer spec-pack learning path from overview pages into Connect, module bundles, and Builder workbench hosting guides.

upgradeSteps.title: Review the updated spec-pack learning path
upgradeSteps.summary: Confirm the new guide pages and cross-links appear in the expected docs navigation flow.
instructions: Open the public docs overview and verify the new learning-path entries for Connect, module bundles, and hosting the Builder workbench.
Follow the related-page links between the spec overview, architecture references, and Studio/Builder guides to confirm the intended navigation loop.

commands: cd packages/bundles/library && bun test && bun run typecheck
cd packages/apps/web-landing && bun run build:types && bun test
evidence: The docs bundle now includes the practical spec-pack pages and related-page metadata needed for a coherent learning-path flow.
The public docs site surfaces those pages on the homepage and route wiring, so readers can traverse the new Connect and Builder-oriented learning path without dead ends.

releases.summary: Fix workflow runtime imports for sandboxed workflow execution and keep workflow authoring on safe subpaths.

audiences.summary: Workflow templates, docs, and devkit code now avoid broad workflow barrel imports in sandboxed workflow entrypoints.
Vercel Workflow projects should keep Node-only code out of "use workflow" modules and use the safe workflow subpaths instead.

deprecations: Importing the broad `@contractspec/lib.contracts-spec/workflow` barrel from sandboxed workflow entrypoints is discouraged.

migrationInstructions.title: Rewrite workflow imports to safe subpaths
migrationInstructions.summary: Keep Node-only workflow runner code out of "use workflow" entrypoints.
migrationInstructions.when: When running ContractSpec workflow specs in Vercel Workflow or other sandboxed workflow runtimes.
steps: Keep Node-only work inside "use step" functions, not the "use workflow" module graph.

upgradeSteps.title: Rewrite workflow imports to safe subpaths
upgradeSteps.summary: Use narrow workflow entrypoints so sandboxed workflow runtimes do not pull Node-only runner code.
instructions: Replace broad workflow barrel imports with the specific `workflow/spec`, `workflow/runner`, `workflow/adapters`, and `workflow/expression` subpaths you need.
Keep `crypto` and other Node-only dependencies in step functions instead of workflow entrypoints.

commands: cd packages/libs/contracts-spec && bun test src/workflow/spec.test.ts src/workflow/validation.test.ts src/workflow/expression.test.ts src/workflow/runner.test.ts
cd packages/integrations/workflow-devkit && bun test && bun run build
cd packages/apps/cli-contractspec && bun test src/templates/workflow.template.test.ts src/templates/workflow-runner.template.test.ts
evidence: Workflow authoring templates now use safe workflow subpaths instead of the broad workflow barrel.
Workflow DevKit runtime imports stay on expression and spec subpaths, avoiding transitive workflow runner bundling in workflow entrypoints.

releases.summary: Pass Bun transpile paths through production JSX mode so published browser bundles stop emitting the dev runtime.

audiences.summary: Shared Bun transpile paths now force production JSX mode, and the affected published browser bundles were rebuilt against that fix.
Browser-facing Contractspec packages no longer publish `react/jsx-dev-runtime` imports in their production JS artifacts.
Production React bundles built from the affected Contractspec packages stop depending on the development JSX runtime.

migrationInstructions.title: Upgrade to the rebuilt package versions
migrationInstructions.summary: Pull the patch releases for the affected Contractspec React/browser packages.
migrationInstructions.when: When a downstream Next or React production bundle was failing on `jsxDEV` paths from published Contractspec packages.
steps: Upgrade the affected Contractspec package versions after this release is published.
Rebuild the downstream production bundle so it picks up the republished artifacts.

upgradeSteps.title: Verify production bundles no longer import the dev JSX runtime
upgradeSteps.summary: Check released JS artifacts for `react/jsx-dev-runtime` imports or `jsxDEV(` call sites.
instructions: Rebuild the affected package set with the updated `@contractspec/tool.bun`.
Scan `dist/` output for `react/jsx-dev-runtime` imports and `jsxDEV(` call sites before publishing.

commands: cd packages/tools/bun && bun test test/transpile-production.test.ts
cd packages/tools/bun && bun test

rg -uuu -l -g '*.js' -e 'from "react/jsx-dev-runtime"' -e "from 'react/jsx-dev-runtime'" -e 'require\("react/jsx-dev-runtime"\)' -e 'jsxDEV\(' packages/libs/**/dist packages/modules/**/dist packages/bundles/**/dist packages/apps/**/dist
evidence: Package `@contractspec/tool.bun` now adds `--production` to both no-bundle and bundled non-watch transpile commands, while watch/dev paths remain unchanged.
The scoped release packages rebuild cleanly against the stricter runtime-relevant scan, with no `react/jsx-dev-runtime` imports or `jsxDEV(` call sites remaining in their built artifacts.

Package `@contractspec/app.cli-contractspec` was excluded from this release because its bundle only contained the literal string `jsxDEV` from embedded TypeScript source text, not a React dev-runtime import path.

releases.summary: Add versioning-backed release capsules, generated patch notes, and guided upgrade flows.

audiences.summary: Release communication is now generated from versioning-backed release capsules and enforced on release branches.
Guided upgrade plans and agent prompts now come from generated upgrade manifests instead of ad hoc prose.
Web changelog consumers can prefer generated release manifests while older package changelogs remain supported as fallback.

deprecations: The standalone release domain under `@contractspec/lib.contracts-spec/release` is deprecated in favor of versioning-owned release metadata.

migrationInstructions.title: Add .release.yaml companions for changesets
migrationInstructions.summary: Published release changesets now require a structured release capsule.
migrationInstructions.when: When preparing release branches, publish workflows, or release communication bundles.
steps: Create `.changeset/<slug>.release.yaml` next to each published changeset.
Record customer impact, migration notes, validation commands, and evidence in the release capsule.
Run `contractspec release build` before `contractspec release check --strict`.

upgradeSteps.title: Scaffold release capsule companions
upgradeSteps.summary: Add release capsules for changesets and include validation evidence.
instructions: Run `contractspec release init` for new release work.
Keep `.changeset/*.md` and `.changeset/*.release.yaml` together in the same PR.
Use `contractspec release brief` or `contractspec upgrade prompt` to generate maintainer, customer, and agent guidance.

upgradeSteps.title: Use generated release manifests in tooling
upgradeSteps.summary: Prefer generated release artifacts for changelog and upgrade flows.
instructions: Run `contractspec release build` to populate `generated/releases/`.
Point changelog or upgrade tooling at `generated/releases/manifest.json` and `generated/releases/upgrade-manifest.json`.

commands: cd packages/libs/contracts-spec && bun test src/versioning/schema.test.ts src/versioning/release-utils.test.ts src/versioning/utils.test.ts src/versioning/refs.test.ts && bun run typecheck && bun run build:bundle && bun run build:types
cd packages/bundles/workspace && bun test src/services/versioning/release-service.test.ts src/services/upgrade/guided-upgrade.test.ts && bun run typecheck && bun run build
cd packages/apps/cli-contractspec && bun run typecheck && bun run build
cd packages/apps/web-landing && bun run build:types
evidence: Release artifacts and upgrade prompts are generated from versioning-backed schemas and bundle.workspace services.
Guided upgrade manifest resolution now honors the current package root inside parent monorepos.
